violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the (back storage area), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
Observed several ceiling tiles above frozen food cooler in poor repair (stained). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

The following food items were observed adulterated: 1) A package of smoked sausage was opened with visible mold growth 2) A package of chips was opened and exposed to rodent droppings PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

The door to the outside, located in the front retail space, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of rodent droppings on and under shelves in the retail area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024
Several ceiling tiles in the retail area were observed with water damage. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025
